Posh.evidence: AI banking chatbot company (Posh AI/Posh Technologies), raised $45M Series B in June 2024, total funding only ~$72.5M — early-stage scale.

PostHog.evidence: Product analytics platform, private, YC-backed, raised Series C funding, valued over $1B.

Postman.evidence: API development platform, private, valued at $5.6B in Series D round.

Postscript.evidence: SMS marketing platform, private, raised Series C funding, valuation in the hundreds of millions to $1B+ range.

PrizePicks.evidence: Daily fantasy sports company, private, valued at approximately $1.5B.

Procurify.evidence: Spend management software, raised $50M USD Series C in Oct 2023, total funding over $70M; described as significant up round.

project44.evidence: Unicorn valued at $2.7B following $80M raise (Nov 2022), total funding of $912M from TPG, Goldman Sachs and others.

Prudential.evidence: Publicly traded large-cap financial services/insurance company (NYSE: PRU).

Qualia.evidence: Real estate closing software, raised $65M Series D in Dec 2020 at over $1B valuation (unicorn).

QuickNode.evidence: Blockchain infrastructure company, raised $60M Series B in Jan 2023 at $800M valuation.

Railway.evidence: Developer infrastructure/hosting startup, private, raised Series B funding, still relatively small/young company.

Ramp.evidence: Corporate card/spend management fintech, private, valued at approximately $16B in recent funding rounds.

Reddit.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE (RDDT) since March 2024.

Redis.evidence: In-memory database company, private, valued at over $2B in prior funding rounds (Series G/late-stage).

Redwood Materials.evidence: Battery recycling/materials company, private, valued at approximately $5B in Series D round.

Relativity Space.evidence: 3D-printed rocket company, private, valued at approximately $4.2B in Series E round.

Remote.com.evidence: Global HR/payroll platform, private, valued at approximately $3B in Series C round.

Render.evidence: Private; raised $100M Series C extension at $1.5B valuation (Feb 2026)

Rent the Runway.evidence: Publicly traded on Nasdaq (RENT), not mega-cap

Replit.evidence: Private AI coding platform, valued at ~$1.16B (2023 raise), later rounds reported higher

Ripple.evidence: Private fintech/crypto co, raised $500M at $40B valuation (Nov 2025)

Ro.evidence: Private telehealth co, valued at $7B (2022 raise, still most recent priced round), ~$1B total raised

Roboflow.evidence: Private computer-vision startup, Series B ($40M raised Nov 2024), total funding only ~$63.6M, valuation undisclosed and modest

Rondo Energy.evidence: Private thermal battery/energy storage startup, Series C funding raising several hundred million

RTX (Raytheon Technologies).evidence: Publicly traded (NYSE: RTX) mega-cap aerospace/defense company, not on mag7 list.

Runway.evidence: Core business is building frontier foundation models for video/image generation; private, valued at $3B (Apr 2025 Series D) rising to $5.3B (2026 Series E)

Safe (Gnosis Safe).evidence: Crypto smart-contract wallet infra, raised $100M in funding (rebrand from Gnosis Safe), secures $100B+ in assets

Saildrone.evidence: Private ocean-drone/autonomous vessel company, Series C+ funding

Salesforce.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE (CRM), large-cap but not among the eight FAANG/Mag7 companies defined for this tier

Salesloft.evidence: Private sales-engagement SaaS, later-stage venture funding (PE investment from Vista/Advent-backed rounds)

Salt Security.evidence: Private API security company, Series D funding at ~$1.4B valuation

Sanity.evidence: Private headless CMS, Series C funding

Sardine.evidence: Private fraud-prevention fintech, Series C funding raising valuation over $1B

Saronic.evidence: Private autonomous-boat defense startup, Series C funding valuing company multiple billions

Saviynt.evidence: Private identity-security company, large PE-backed funding round (TPG-led, ~$2B+ valuation)

Scale AI.evidence: Private data-labeling/AI infra company, valued at $14B+ after Meta's 2024 investment

SeatGeek.evidence: Private ticketing marketplace, Series E funding, valuation over $1B

Secureframe.evidence: Private compliance-automation startup, latest round is Series B ($56M, Feb 2022) at $294-350M valuation, no Series C found

Semgrep.evidence: Private code-security company (formerly r2c), Series C/D funding

Sentry.evidence: Private developer-tools company, Series F funding at ~$3B valuation

Shield AI.evidence: Private defense-tech (autonomous systems), Series F funding, valuation ~$5.3B

Shift Technology.evidence: Private insurtech (fraud/claims AI), Series E funding

Sierra.evidence: Private AI customer-agent startup founded by Bret Taylor, valued at ~$10B in 2025 funding rounds

Sigma Computing.evidence: Private BI/analytics company, Series D funding, valuation over $1.5B

SingleStore.evidence: Private distributed-database company, late-stage funding (Series F/G)

Skydio.evidence: Private drone company, Series E funding, valuation ~$2.2B

SnapLogic.evidence: Private integration-platform company, later-stage venture funding (Series F+)

Socket.evidence: Private supply-chain security startup, Series C funding at $1B valuation (2026)

Socure.evidence: Private identity-verification company, Series E/F funding, valuation ~$4.5B

SonarSource.evidence: Private code-quality/security company, PE-backed (Advent/EQT/BlackRock investments) at multi-billion valuation

SoundCloud.evidence: Private music platform, $850M valuation per 2024 SiriusXM minority investment reporting

Span.evidence: Private home-electrification/smart panel startup, Series D funding

Spotify.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE (SPOT), not a mega-cap (~$100B range), music streaming tech company.

Squarespace.evidence: Formerly public (NYSE:SQSP); taken private by Permira in 2024 in a deal valuing it at roughly $6.6-6.9B.

Starburst.evidence: Private data-query company; Series D round valued it at ~$3.35B (2021).

Stitch Fix.evidence: Publicly traded on NASDAQ (SFIX), small-cap e-commerce/tech company.

Strava.evidence: Private fitness-social app; last reported valuation ~$1.5B.

Stripe.evidence: Private payments company; internal tender offer valuations put it around $85-91.5B as of 2024-2025, far past Series C threshold.

Sumo Logic.evidence: Formerly public (NASDAQ:SUMO); taken private by Francisco Partners in May 2023 for $1.7B.

Supabase.evidence: Private open-source database platform; Series D/E rounds pushed valuation to roughly $2B (2024).

Synthesia.evidence: Private AI video company; Series D (2024) valued it at $2.1B.

Sysdig.evidence: Private cloud security company; Series F (2021) valued it at ~$1.19B.

T-Mobile.evidence: Publicly traded mega-cap telecom on NASDAQ (TMUS), not in the specified mag7 list.

Tanium.evidence: Private endpoint security company; historically valued near $9B (2021 round), remains private.

Target.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE (TGT), large-cap retailer.

Temporal.evidence: Private workflow-orchestration company (Temporal Technologies); Series C (2022) valued it at ~$1.5B.

thatgamecompany (Sky: Children of the Li.evidence: Raised $160M growth investment led by TPG in March 2022 to expand Sky: Children of the Light into a metaverse experience; large late-stage private raise from a growth-equity firm.

Thoughtworks.evidence: Formerly public (NASDAQ:TWKS); taken private by Apax Funds in a $1.75B deal completed November 2024.

Thumbtack.evidence: Private services marketplace; 2021 round valued it at ~$3.2B.

Tinder.evidence: Wholly owned brand of publicly traded Match Group (NASDAQ:MTCH), which sets comp practices for its brands.

Tines.evidence: Private security-automation company; Series C (2024) valued it at ~$1.25B.

Toast.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE (TOST), restaurant tech company.

Torq.evidence: Private security-automation company; Series C (2024) valued it at ~$1.75B.

Trace3.evidence: Private IT consultancy/reseller acquired by Apollo Funds in 2025 for ~$1.65B, ~$1.4B annual revenue scale.

Trainline.evidence: Publicly traded on London Stock Exchange (LSE:TRN), UK rail-ticketing tech company.

Transmit Security.evidence: Private identity-security company; 2021 round valued it at ~$2.2B.

Truveta.evidence: Private healthcare data company; Series C (Jan 2025) brought valuation above $1B, reaching unicorn status.

Turnkey.evidence: Private crypto wallet-infrastructure startup; only reached Series B ($30M, 2025) with ~$50M total raised and undisclosed but modest valuation.

Twilio.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE (TWLO), communications-API tech company.

Twitch.evidence: Wholly owned subsidiary of Amazon; compensation typically follows Amazon's structure.

UiPath.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE (PATH), enterprise automation software company.

Valtech.evidence: Private PE-backed digital agency; BC Partners investment valued it at ~$1.4B (2021).

Vannevar Labs.evidence: Private defense-tech company; October 2024 valuation reached ~$1.5B.

Vanta.evidence: Private security-compliance company; Series C (2024) valued it at ~$2.45B.

Varda Space Industries.evidence: Private in-space manufacturing company; Series C/D rounds valued it at ~$1.58B.

Vercel.evidence: Private web-infrastructure company; Series E and later rounds valued it in the multi-billion-dollar range (>$3B).

Veriff.evidence: Private identity-verification company; Series C (2022) valued it at ~$1.5B.

Verizon.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE (VZ), large-cap telecom.

Verkada.evidence: Private physical-security company; Series F (2024) valued it at ~$4.5B.

Via.evidence: Via Transportation completed its US IPO, pricing at $46/share, valuing the company at ~$3.65B and listing on NYSE under VIA.

Virta Health.evidence: Private digital-health company; Series E (2021) valued it at ~$2B.

Visa.evidence: Publicly traded mega-cap on NYSE (V), payments network, not in the specified mag7 list.

Voodoo.evidence: Private French mobile-gaming company; 2022 valuation reached ~$2.31B, with Tencent and GBL as investors.

VTS.evidence: Private commercial real-estate software company; Series E (Sept 2022) valued it at ~$1.7B.

Waabi.evidence: Private autonomous-trucking company; reached unicorn status via a $750M Series C round and Uber-backed milestone capital (2026).

Warner Bros Discovery.evidence: Publicly traded on NASDAQ (WBD), large media/entertainment company.

Waymo.evidence: Alphabet subsidiary that raises its own external capital and valuation; latest round (Feb 2026) valued it at $126B, led by outside investors alongside Alphabet.

Wealthfront.evidence: Private robo-advisor; last primary valuation ~$1.4B (2021), remains private.

Weaviate.evidence: Private vector-database company; closed a Series C round (Oct 2025), satisfying the Series C+ private-company criterion.

Whoop.evidence: Raised $575M Series G at $10.1B valuation (March 2026), led by Collaborative Fund.

Workato.evidence: Raised $200M Series E at $5.7B valuation (2021); secondary markets in 2025 suggested valuation nearer $1.7B, still well above growth-stage threshold.

WorkOS.evidence: Raised $100M Series C at $2B valuation, led by Meritech and Sapphire.

Writer.evidence: Raised $200M Series C at $1.9B valuation (Nov 2024); enterprise generative-AI platform building proprietary Palmyra models but not a frontier/foundation-model lab on the scale of OpenAI/Anthropic.

Yotpo.evidence: Raised $230M Series F at $1.4B valuation (2021), later valuation cited near $2.5B with Shopify investment.

Zapier.evidence: Private company valued at $5B (2021 post-money, some estimates up to $7B); $310M revenue in 2024, capital-efficient bootstrapped growth.

Zeta Global.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE under ticker ZETA, market cap approximately $5B.

Zip.evidence: Raised $190M Series D at $2.2B valuation (Oct 2024), led by BOND.

Zipline.evidence: Raised $600M+ (Series H expanded to $800M) at $7.6B valuation (Jan-Mar 2026).

ZipRecruiter.evidence: Publicly traded on NYSE under ticker ZIP, market cap approximately $320M as of July 2026.

Zocdoc.evidence: Post-money valuation of $2.62B as of Series D-2/E round in Feb 2021; has raised $1.15B total, remains private with no IPO.

ZoomInfo.evidence: Publicly traded on NASDAQ under ticker GTM, market cap approximately $0.85B as of June 2026.

Zoox.evidence: Acquired by Amazon in 2020 for $1.2B and continues to operate as an Amazon-owned autonomous-vehicle subsidiary, aligning its comp structure with Amazon (RSUs etc.).

Zopa.evidence: UK digital bank raised $87M at valuation exceeding $1B (Dec 2024), reaffirming unicorn status; considering but has not completed an IPO.

Zuora.evidence: Formerly public (NYSE:ZUO); taken private by Silver Lake/GIC in a $1.7B deal completed Feb 2025 and delisted.

Zwift.evidence: Raised $450M Series C in 2020 at valuation north of $1B, current valuation cited around $1B.

unclearCompanies.evidence: Public mega-cap (NYSE: MMM) but a diversified industrial/manufacturing conglomerate, not a tech/tech-adjacent company — doesn't fit the tier's intent.
Public mega-cap healthcare/medical-devices company (NYSE: ABT), not tech/tech-adjacent.
Wholly-owned subsidiary of publicly traded Booking Holdings, not a standalone comp-comparable entity.
Public mega-cap insurance company (NYSE: ALL), not tech/tech-adjacent.
Formerly public (NYSE: PLAN); taken private by Thoma Bravo for $10.7B in 2022. Now a mature PE-owned company without typical venture-growth comp structures — an 'old large private co' case.
Formerly public (NASDAQ: ASTR) small-launch space company; taken private by its founders in a $0.50/share buyout in July 2024 after a sharp decline — atypical comp comparable.
Public mega-cap telecom (NYSE: T); large but slow-growth legacy telecom, not a fit for tech-oriented comp tiers.

Mature cybersecurity company owned by private-equity firms Francisco Partners (majority, since 2018) and Clearlake Capital (minority, since 2021); an 'old large private co' PE buyout without typical venture-growth comp structure.
Fintech cash-advance app being acquired by Upbound Group in a deal worth up to $460M (announced 2024/closing ~Q1 2025); no longer an independent private company with its own comp market.

Formerly public spend-management company taken private by Thoma Bravo in an $8B buyout completed February 2023; no longer public or venture-backed, so it doesn't cleanly fit the growth-stage-private VC framework.
Wholly owned subsidiary of Sony (via Sony Pictures Entertainment/Crunchyroll LLC since 2021), not an independently valued startup with its own comp market.
Bootstrapped cloud-consulting firm (~200 employees, ~$40M ARR) with no disclosed VC funding rounds or valuation — insufficient evidence to place into a funding-stage tier.
European ride-hailing joint venture, formerly owned by BMW/Mercedes, acquired by Lyft for ~$199M in April 2025; foreign subsidiary with no independent US comp analog.

unclearCompanies.company: GDIT (General Dynamics Information Technology, subsidiary of General Dynamics)
unclearCompanies.evidence: Wholly-owned government-IT-services subsidiary of General Dynamics Corp; per rules, government contractor subsidiaries are classified as unclear rather than guessed.
GovTech is a statutory board of the Singapore government (under the Prime Minister's Office), not a private-sector company, so there is no comparable comp-market tier.
Publicly traded (NASDAQ: HAS) but a toy/entertainment company, not a tech or tech-adjacent business, so it doesn't fit the defined tiers well.
Meal-kit company acquired by and operated as a subsidiary of Kroger since 2018; not an independent company with its own comp market.
Private Ukraine-based IT outsourcing/staffing company with over 3,000 employees; no disclosed VC funding rounds or valuation, and largely a foreign staffing firm without a clear US comp analog.
Private IT/software consulting firm (~500-1,000 employees) with no disclosed VC funding rounds or valuation data available.
Large, long-established private developer-tools company that is self-funded/bootstrapped with no disclosed VC rounds or public valuation, fitting the 'old large private co' unclear case.
Mega-cap public company (NYSE: JNJ) but a pharmaceutical/healthcare business, not tech or tech-adjacent, so it does not fit the defined comp tiers.

Publicly traded (NASDAQ: LCID) EV maker — evidence and tier conflict (source flags it as not mega-cap tech, but was mistakenly tiered faang-mag7); insufficient consistent evidence to place confidently.
Digital-services/consulting firm with a minority PE growth investment from JLL Partners; no disclosed valuation or funding stage comparable to a product startup.

Delisted from NYSE in May 2025 after an all-cash take-private acquisition by the Nordstrom family and Liverpool ($24.25/share); no longer a public comp or venture-style private company, so tier doesn't map cleanly.
Ad/design agency, recently sold by Interpublic Group back to management and PE firm Truelink Capital (March 2025); not a typical VC-funded tech startup, no clear comp-tier analog.
Reply S.p.A. is an Italian IT consulting firm publicly traded on Borsa Italiana; no direct US comp-market analog
Wholly-owned subsidiary of Tencent since 2015 (100% stake); no standalone comp data as an independent company
Small private IT consultancy (~150 employees), no disclosed funding rounds or valuation data found
Wholly-owned game studio subsidiary of publicly traded Take-Two Interactive; no standalone comp data
Formerly public (ROVR); taken private by Blackstone in a $2.3B all-cash deal (Feb 2024), now PE-owned with no independent comp benchmark
Acquired by Savvy Games Group (Saudi PIF-backed) for $4.9B in 2023; now a subsidiary, not independently comp-benchmarked
Sequoia One is a PEO/HR-services offering from VC firm Sequoia Capital, not an independent operating company with its own comp market
Private, owned by private-equity firms Francisco Partners and Vista Equity Partners since 2017; no venture-style valuation/comp data available
Finnish private IT/digital consultancy; foreign company with no direct US comp analog
Merged ad agency unit fully owned by publicly traded WPP plc (LSE:WPP); no standalone US comp analog or independent valuation data.
Private photo-editing app; last raised a Series B in 2019 with a reported ~$0.6B valuation, but funding data is stale (no rounds since) making current comp tier uncertain.
Acquired by DoorDash in 2022 (~$2.8-8.1B deal); now operates as a DoorDash subsidiary in Europe/Middle East with no independent US comp benchmark.
Acquired by CrunchTime! in 2022 (undisclosed terms); now rebranded as Crunchtime Ops Execution with no independent standalone comp benchmark.
